Zero Hunger.reason: The course explicitly identifies "goal 2 zero hunger" as a key world development issue it will cover within the context of foreign aid and development.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: Explicitly lists "goal 7 affordable and renewable energy" and discusses ending fossil energy use, aligning with Targets 7.2 and 7.a.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course covers "industry, innovation and infrastructure" and readings on "Digital Innovation in Government" support targets on infrastructure and technology (9.a, 9.c).

Climate Action.reason: Course objectives focus on the "Paris Agreement" and "COP 28", and readings cover "Climate Finance", directly addressing Targets 13.2 and 13.a.

Life on Land.reason: Explicitly lists "goal 15 on the land" and a reading on "Biodiversity and Development Finance" directly supports mobilizing resources for biodiversity (Target 15.a).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Explicitly lists "goal 16 peace and justice" and readings on "Inclusive Governance" and "Reinforcing Democracy" directly support building effective institutions (Targets 16.6, 16.7).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course title "Foreign Aid and Development" and readings like "Multilateral Development Finance 2024" are the definition of this goal, especially Targets 17.2, 17.3, 17.16.
